code:
def longestPalindromicSubsequence(s: str) -> int:
    n = len(s)

    if n == 0:
        return 0
    
    # dp[i][j] will store the length of the longest palindromic subsequence of the substring s[i...j]
    dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]

    # All substrings of length 1 are palindromes of length 1
    for i in range(n):
        dp[i][i] = 1

    # Fill the table for lengths from 2 to n
    # 'length' represents the current substring length (j - i + 1)
    for length in range(2, n + 1):
        # 'i' is the starting index of the current substring
        for i in range(n - length + 1):
            # 'j' is the ending index of the current substring
            j = i + length - 1

            # Case 1: If the characters at the ends of the current substring match
            if s[i] == s[j]:
                # The length of the LPS for s[i...j] is 2 (for s[i] and s[j])
                # plus the LPS of the inner substring s[i+1...j-1].
                # If length is 2 (e.g., "aa"), i+1 > j-1 (e.g., i=0, j=1 => 1 > 0).
                # In such cases, dp[i+1][j-1] would refer to an empty or invalid substring,
                # which is correctly initialized to 0 in our dp table.
                dp[i][j] = 2 + dp[i+1][j-1]
            # Case 2: If the characters at the ends of the current substring do not match
            else:
                # We cannot include both s[i] and s[j] in the palindrome.
                # So, we take the maximum of:
                # 1. LPS of s[i+1...j] (excluding s[i])
                # 2. LPS of s[i...j-1] (excluding s[j])
                dp[i][j] = max(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1])
    
    # The result is the LPS of the entire string s[0...n-1]
    return dp[0][n-1]
